items.title: Supervised learning pipeline
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  data["labeled data (x, y)"]
  train["train f_theta"]
  eval["evaluate on held-out (x, y)"]
  infer["predict y_hat for new x"]
  data --> train --> eval --> infer
items.caption: Labeled examples drive training; evaluation still needs labels on held-out data; inference uses only x.

items.title: Supervision signal
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  x["input features x"]
  model["model f_theta"]
  yhat["prediction y_hat"]
  y["true label y"]
  loss["loss L(y_hat, y)"]
  x --> model --> yhat
  yhat --> loss
  y --> loss
  loss --> update["update theta"]
items.caption: The label y is the supervision signal: loss compares ŷ to y and drives parameter updates.

items.title: Supervised among learning paradigms
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  sl["Supervised: labeled (x, y)"]
  usl["Unsupervised: x only"]
  ssl["Self-supervised: y from x"]
  rl["Reinforcement: reward signal"]
  sl --- usl
  sl --- ssl
  sl --- rl
items.caption: Supervised learning is defined by explicit per-example labels y paired with inputs x.
